panels.description: Request success rate. Green if >= 99.5%, red if < 98%. Only requests with finished_reason='stop' or 'length' count as successful.

targets.expr: (sum(rate(vllm:request_success_total{finished_reason=~"stop|length", namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m])) / clamp_min(sum(rate(vllm:request_success_total{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m])), 0.001)) * 100

panels.title: Success Rate

panels.description: Current Running + Waiting requests. A sudden spike indicates a traffic surge.

targets.expr: sum(vllm:num_requests_running{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}) + sum(vllm:num_requests_waiting{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"})

panels.title: Active Requests

panels.description: Scheduler efficiency: Running / (Running + Waiting). 100% means all requests are processed immediately. Below 70% indicates overload.

targets.expr: clamp_max((sum(vllm:num_requests_running{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}) + 0.001) / (sum(vllm:num_requests_running{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}) + sum(vllm:num_requests_waiting{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}) + 0.001), 1)

panels.title: System Efficiency

panels.description: P99 time to first token. Key metric for perceived initial response speed. Ideally under 1 second.
panels.title: TTFT P99

panels.description: [Critical] Preemption rate. Running requests evicted due to KV cache shortage. Any value > 0 requires immediate attention — consider increasing GPU memory or reducing batch size.
panels.title: Preemption Rate

panels.description: End-to-end latency from request arrival to response completion. P50 = median, P99 = tail latency. A P99 spike signals a bottleneck.
panels.title: E2E Request Latency

panels.description: Prefill (input processing) vs Decode (output generation) time breakdown. High prefill = long input bottleneck; high decode = long output bottleneck.

targets.expr: rate(vllm:request_prefill_time_seconds_sum{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m]) / clamp_min(rate(vllm:request_prefill_time_seconds_count{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m]), 0.001)

rate(vllm:request_decode_time_seconds_sum{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m]) / clamp_min(rate(vllm:request_decode_time_seconds_count{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m]), 0.001)

panels.title: Inference Stage Breakdown

panels.description: TTFT (Time To First Token) vs TPOT (Time Per Output Token). High TTFT = prefill bottleneck; high TPOT = decode bottleneck.
panels.title: TTFT vs TPOT

panels.description: Tokens processed per second. Input = prompt tokens, Output = generation tokens. Higher throughput indicates better system efficiency.
panels.title: Token Throughput

panels.description: Input-to-output token ratio. High = long input + short output (RAG); low = short input + long output (generation/summarization).

targets.expr: sum(rate(vllm:prompt_tokens_total{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m])) / clamp_min(sum(rate(vllm:generation_tokens_total{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m])), 0.001)

panels.title: Token I/O Ratio

panels.description: Compute savings from prefix cache. Higher = more prefill computation skipped, saving GPU resources. Formula: (1 - computed/total_input) * 100.

targets.expr: (1 - (sum(rate(vllm:request_prefill_kv_computed_tokens_sum{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m])) / clamp_min(sum(rate(vllm:prompt_tokens_total{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m])), 1))) * 100

panels.title: Prefix Cache Savings

panels.description: Distribution of input (prompt) token counts per request. Darker = more requests at that length. Concentrated bands suggest optimization targets.
panels.title: Request Length Heatmap (Prompt)

panels.description: Time requests spend in the queue before processing. Increases when GPU is busy. Persistently high values suggest scaling out.
panels.title: Request Queue Time

panels.description: GPU KV cache utilization. Stores intermediate computation results. Above 80% = caution; above 95% = preemption risk.
panels.title: GPU KV Cache Usage

panels.description: Prefix cache hit rate. Reuse ratio of system prompts and few-shot examples. Above 70% is efficient. Key metric for RAG workloads.

targets.expr: sum(rate(vllm:prefix_cache_hits_total{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m])) / clamp_min(sum(rate(vllm:prefix_cache_queries_total{namespace=~"$namespace", job="$job_filter", model_name="$model_name", instance=~"$vllm_instance"}[5m])), 1)

panels.title: Prefix Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Requests by scheduler state. Running = actively inferring on GPU, Waiting = queued, Swapped = evicted to CPU memory. Sustained Waiting growth = overload.
panels.title: Scheduler State

panels.description: Python GC frequency and process memory usage. Frequent GC or steadily rising RSS may indicate a memory leak.
panels.title: Python GC & Memory

panels.description: Request finish reason distribution. stop = normal completion (EOS), length = max tokens reached, abort = cancelled. High abort ratio indicates a problem.
panels.title: Finish Reason Distribution

panels.description: Total request throughput vs successful requests over time. A gap between the two lines indicates failed requests.
panels.title: Throughput vs Success
